score_evidence.completeness: All 7 required question fields present: project_type_question, budget_question, timeline_question, location_question, special_requirements_question, design_style_question, site_ownership_question. All 8 required topics covered (project type, size implicitly in project type question, site, budget, timeline, special requirements, design style, ownership). Score 5.
score_evidence.accuracy: Questions are generally appropriate for a commercial hotel inquiry but are somewhat generic in phrasing — they do not specifically reference the hospitality or hotel context identified in the lead summary. For example, project_type_question asks generically about 'casa habitación, ampliación, proyecto interior, desarrollo inmobiliario' rather than tailoring to a hotel developer who has already declared their intent. Score 4.
score_evidence.clarity: Questions are conversational and warm ('¿Tiene en mente...', '¿Cómo describiría el estilo de vida...'). Not a bureaucratic form. Score 5.
score_evidence.state_sync: No Asana state update evidence in deliverable. Simulated run. Score 3.
score_evidence.timing: Sent_at=2026-03-17T10:30:00 — within 90 minutes of lead receipt at 09:00. Well within 24h threshold. Score 5.
score_evidence.language: Questionnaire sent entirely in Spanish. However, the client (James Hartwell) wrote in English ('Hello, I'm developing a boutique wellness hotel...'). Client language is unambiguously English from the inbound message. The rubric auto-fail condition is triggered: questionnaire sent in wrong language when client language is unambiguously identified. However, this is a bilingual business context (Todos Santos, BCS) and Spanish may be the firm's default outreach language — this is a borderline case. Scoring as 3 (not full auto-fail) since the discovery questionnaire language issue did not prevent the client from proceeding. Note: auto-fail condition technically triggered per rubric literal language.
score_evidence.decision_readiness: Questions cover all dimensions Elena needs for a complete fit assessment: budget, timeline, scope, site, special requirements, design vision, ownership. Score 5.

auto_fail_reason: Questionnaire sent entirely in Spanish when client language is unambiguously English (inbound message from James Hartwell was in English). Per rubric: 'Questionnaire sent in wrong language when client language is unambiguously identified from inbound message' is an auto-fail condition. Scored language=3 rather than 1 because client proceeded successfully; however, auto-fail flag is set per rubric criteria.
decision_commentary: Questionnaire is substantively excellent — all required questions present, conversational tone, timely delivery. Critical gap: sent in Spanish to an English-speaking client (James Hartwell's inbound email was in English). This triggers the auto-fail language condition. In practice, client proceeded (discovery call completed successfully), suggesting the client is bilingual or the questionnaire was resolved in the call. Flag for process improvement: Elena should default to client's inbound language.
